preview_text:
The model assesses Algeria as favorite with a 38% win probability, while Jordan carries a 30% chance of victory. The remaining probability concentrates on a draw at 31%, indicating a competitive match where no outcome is heavily favored. Algeria edges ahead by roughly 8 percentage points in outright win odds.

The W/D/L spread reflects a tight contest. A draw sits between the two outcomes and carries near-parity with Jordan's win probability, suggesting the match could resolve via any of the three routes with material likelihood. The gap between Algeria's win chance and a draw is modest—7 percentage points—which points to a closely matched encounter rather than a one-sided affair.

The sportsbook consensus diverges notably from the model. Books assign Algeria a 53% win probability, roughly 15 percentage points higher than the model's 38%, and price Jordan at just 20% compared to the model's 30%. The market tightens the draw to 26%. This spread suggests bookmakers view Algeria as a stronger favorite than the statistical framework does. The dashboard makes no claim about which assessment is more reliable; the gap merits scrutiny.
